version.description: Revision number of this offer within its application. Greenhouse creates a new offer row (incrementing `version`) whenever a tracked field on an existing offer changes — typically `starts_on`, `opening_id`, or a custom field configured to trigger a new version. Pair with `current_only=true` to filter the list endpoint down to the latest version per application.

application_id.description: Id of the application this offer is extended on. Every offer belongs to exactly one application; the offer is voided if the application is rejected or deleted.

job_id.description: Id of the job this offer's application is on.

candidate_id.description: Id of the candidate (person) receiving this offer. Resolved through the offer's application.

opening_id.description: Id of the specific opening this offer is being extended for. `null` when the offer has not yet been linked to an opening.

status.description: Lifecycle status of the offer. `Created` for offers still being drafted or pending approval, `Accepted` once the candidate accepts, `Rejected` if declined or withdrawn, and `Deprecated` for superseded prior versions (a new offer version replaces an earlier one with this status).

starts_on.description: Candidate's proposed start date, in ISO 8601 (YYYY-MM-DD). `null` when no start date has been set on the offer.

sent_on.description: Date the offer was sent to the candidate, in ISO 8601 (YYYY-MM-DD). `null` until the offer has been sent.

resolved_at.description: Timestamp the offer was resolved (`Accepted` or `Rejected`), in ISO 8601. Date updates submitted through `PATCH /v3/offers/{id}` are normalized to noon UTC on the supplied date. `null` while the offer is still `Created` or has been superseded as `Deprecated` without a resolution.

description: Schema for the 'offers' stream, linked to the '/v3/offers' endpoint.
